input.prompt:
Please do the following in the browser:

1. Navigate to https://news.ycombinator.com (use browser_open if needed — the browser may already be in use by another session, in which case just call browser_open with the URL to navigate the existing tab).
2. Take a screenshot of the homepage.
3. Click on the first story link (the title of the topmost story on the page, not "Hacker News" itself, not the points/comments). It's the first link in the first row of the story list.
4. Wait for the page to load, then take another screenshot.
5. Report back:
   - The title of the page you landed on
   - The current URL
   - A brief description of what's on the page

Do NOT close the browser when you're done. Report the final URL clearly at the end.

content.text:
The browser is currently locked by another active session (ID: fc845c0b-f276-4f45-ba15-6b6145a8435c). None of my browser tools can access it while that session holds ownership.

Here is what I can tell you:

- The browser is in use by a different session and cannot be accessed right now.
- All browser tool calls (open, screenshot, snapshot, eval) are returning the same ownership error.

To resolve this, you would need to either:

1. Wait for the other session to complete and release the browser.
2. Terminate the existing session so this one can take ownership.
3. If you are running this in a multi-agent setup, ensure the parent agent releases the browser before delegating to this sub-agent.

Once the browser becomes available, I can proceed with all the steps: navigating to Hacker News, taking screenshots, clicking the first story, and reporting back the page title, URL, and description.
